title: Calophaca nigricans
intro: Calophaca nigricans, also known as the Mongolian calophaca, is a small shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calophaca nigricans include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Mongolian medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas and tinctures to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Calophaca nigricans is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of China and is often found growing at high elevations. Calophaca nigricans is a deciduous shrub or small tree, known for its dark bark and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant is classified as belonging to the subfamily Faboideae and is closely related to other species within the same family. Further classification of Calophaca nigricans at the genus and species level is still a topic of ongoing research and study.
